asset.name: asset

arch.name: arch
arch.title: Architecture this OS is running on

eol.name: eol
eol.title: Asset platform end-of-life status
eol.desc: End-of-life metadata for the asset's detected platform: the EoL `date`, a vendor product-page URL, and a documentation URL. Audits use this to flag hosts running platforms whose vendor has stopped issuing security patches.

family.name: family
family.title: List of platform families that this platform belongs to

kind.name: kind
kind.title: High-level platform category (e.g., api, baremetal, virtualmachine, container, container-image, network)

labels.name: labels
labels.title: Labels attached to the asset by its platform or cloud provider (for example cloud resource tags or Kubernetes labels)

name.name: name
name.title: Human readable name of the asset

platform.name: platform
platform.title: Platform for this asset (redhat, windows, k8s-pod)

platformMetadata.name: platformMetadata
platformMetadata.title: Platform metadata such as distribution and release details

runtime.name: runtime
runtime.title: Specific runtime the asset is operating in (e.g., docker-container, podman-container, aws-ec2-instance)

title.name: title
title.title: Human-readable title of the platform (e.g., "Red Hat 8, Container")

asset.title: Asset identity and platform metadata
asset.desc: Identity and platform context for the asset under evaluation: its human-readable name, its identifiers, and the detected platform, version, and architecture. The `kind` field classifies the asset (api, baremetal, virtualmachine, container, container-image, or network) and `runtime` records where it runs (docker-container, podman-container, aws-ec2-instance, and so on). This is the root context most audits branch from, so you can scope checks by platform, kind, or runtime before querying deeper resources.

asset.eol.name: asset.eol
asset.eol.title: Asset platform end-of-life status
asset.eol.desc: End-of-life metadata for the asset's detected platform: the EoL `date`, a vendor product-page URL, and a documentation URL. Audits use this to flag hosts running platforms whose vendor has stopped issuing security patches.

cpe.name: cpe

other.name: other
other.title: Miscellaneous CPE attributes not covered by the standard fields

part.name: part
part.title: CPE part designation: a (application), h (hardware), or o (operating system)

cpe.title: Common Platform Enumeration (CPE)
cpe.desc: Parsed CPE identifier, NIST NVD's structured naming scheme for products. Select one with `cpe(uri: "cpe:2.3:a:vendor:product:1.0:...")` to break the CPE down into its part designation (a / h / o), vendor, product, version, update, edition, language, software edition, target software, target hardware, and miscellaneous fields, so you can correlate an asset against vulnerability advisories keyed by CPE.

mondoo.name: mondoo

build.name: build
build.title: Build identifier of the client (e.g., production, development, or commit hash)

jobEnvironment.name: jobEnvironment
jobEnvironment.title: Execution environment the agent detected
jobEnvironment.desc: Dict describing the context this evaluation runs in. Keys: `id` is the environment namespace (e.g., client.mondoo.com for a local run, codebuild.aws.amazon.com, or devops.azure.com), and `name` is the human-readable label (e.g., Mondoo Client, AWS CodeBuild, or Azure Pipelines).

version.name: version
version.title: Version of the client running on the asset

mondoo.title: MQL runtime and execution environment
mondoo.desc: Identity of the client running the current MQL evaluation: the client version and build identifier, the architecture string, the connection capabilities reported for the active connection, and the `jobEnvironment` dict describing the execution context the agent detected (a local Mondoo Client shell, or a CI/CD system such as GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, AWS CodeBuild, or Azure Pipelines).

parse.name: parse
parse.title: Common parsers
parse.desc: Namespace for cross-cutting parsing helpers that apply to any connection. `parse.date(value, format)` reads a `time` from a string and an optional layout (for example `parse.date('2023/12/23', '2006/01/02')`, or a named layout like `rfc3339` or `ansic`), and `parse.duration(value)` converts a duration string into a `time` interval.

product.name: product

releaseCycleInformation.name: releaseCycleInformation
releaseCycleInformation.title: End of life information for a product release

product.title: Product end-of-life lookup
product.desc: End-of-life and support metadata for a named software product, resolved against Mondoo's product catalog. Select a product by its `name` and `version`, then read `releaseCycle` for the matching release cycle, including the latest version, first and last release dates, end of active support, end of life, and end of extended support. Useful for flagging assets that run unsupported or soon-to-be-unsupported software.

product.releaseCycleInformation.name: product.releaseCycleInformation

name.name: name
name.title: Name identifier of the release cycle

product.releaseCycleInformation.title: End of life information for a product release

regex.name: regex
regex.title: Built-in regular expressions
regex.desc: Library of pre-defined regex patterns for common formats, ready to use in matching and validation without writing your own expression. Covers IPv4 / IPv6 addresses, HTTP/HTTPS URLs, email addresses, MAC addresses, hyphen-delimited UUIDs, emoji code points, semantic version numbers, and credit card numbers.

time.name: time

now.name: now
now.title: The current time on the local system

today.name: today
today.title: The current day starting at midnight

tomorrow.name: tomorrow
tomorrow.title: The next day starting at midnight

time.title: Date and time helpers
time.desc: Helpers for time-aware MQL expressions. `now` returns the current local time; `today` and `tomorrow` return midnight on those days; `second`, `minute`, `hour`, and `day` are unit constants used when computing durations.

unicode.name: unicode
unicode.title: Unicode text classification
unicode.desc: Unicode-classified view of an input string, selected with `unicode(input: "...")`. The `classification` field gives a per-character breakdown (each character's code point, category, and description); `categories` gives a per-category histogram of how many characters fall into each Unicode general category. Useful for detecting homoglyph or hidden-character attacks in identifiers, filenames, and configuration values.

category.name: category
category.title: Unicode general category code
category.desc: Two-letter Unicode general category code. One of Lu, Ll, Lt, Lm, Lo (letters), Mn, Mc, Me (marks), Nd, Nl, No (numbers), Pc, Pd, Ps, Pe, Pi, Pf, Po (punctuation), Sm, Sc, Sk, So (symbols), Zs, Zl, Zp (separators), or Cc, Cf, Cs, Co, Cn (other).

count.name: count
count.title: Number of characters in the input that fall into this category

majorCategory.name: majorCategory
majorCategory.title: Major Unicode category
majorCategory.desc: Single-letter major Unicode category: L (letter), M (mark), N (number), P (punctuation), S (symbol), Z (separator), or C (other).

category.name: category
category.title: Unicode general category code
category.desc: Two-letter Unicode general category code. One of Lu, Ll, Lt, Lm, Lo (letters), Mn, Mc, Me (marks), Nd, Nl, No (numbers), Pc, Pd, Ps, Pe, Pi, Pf, Po (punctuation), Sm, Sc, Sk, So (symbols), Zs, Zl, Zp (separators), or Cc, Cf, Cs, Co, Cn (other).

char.name: char
char.title: Character is the actual Unicode character as a string

codePoint.name: codePoint
codePoint.title: CodePoint is the Unicode code point in U+XXXX format

description.name: description
description.title: Description is the human-readable description of the category

majorCategory.name: majorCategory
majorCategory.title: Major Unicode category
majorCategory.desc: Single-letter major Unicode category: L (letter), M (mark), N (number), P (punctuation), S (symbol), Z (separator), or C (other).

position.name: position
position.title: Position is the zero-based index of the character in the original string

uuid.name: uuid

urn.name: urn
urn.title: RFC 2141 URN form of the UUID (e.g., urn:uuid:xxxxxxxx-...)

variant.name: variant
variant.title: Variant encoded in the UUID
variant.desc: One of RFC4122, Reserved (NCS backward compatibility), Microsoft (backward compatibility), Future, or Invalid.

uuid.title: UUID (RFC 4122 / DCE 1.1)
uuid.desc: Universally unique identifier parsed from its canonical string. Initialize with `uuid(value: "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx")` to read the canonical string representation, the URN form (`urn:uuid:...`), the version (1, 4, 5, and so on), and the variant.

exchange.name: exchange
exchange.title: Vulnerability Exchange information (experimental)
exchange.desc: Single vulnerability record as published by an upstream feed. The `id` field selects the vulnerability identifier (e.g., CVE-2025-12345) and `source` names the registry that published the record (such as NVD or GHSA). Use it to reference a specific advisory when correlating security findings across feeds.

vulnerability.exchange.name: vulnerability.exchange

source.name: source
source.title: Source/registry that published the vulnerability record (e.g., NVD, GHSA)

vulnerability.exchange.title: Vulnerability Exchange information (experimental)
vulnerability.exchange.desc: Single vulnerability record as published by an upstream feed. The `id` field selects the vulnerability identifier (e.g., CVE-2025-12345) and `source` names the registry that published the record (such as NVD or GHSA). Use it to reference a specific advisory when correlating security findings across feeds.
